handoff_focus: restore fair participation and workable process control before substantive option work continues

escalation.rationale: Co-handling is required because the state reflects a fairness or repeated-process breakdown that needs live human mediation support.

summary_state.next_step: Use the next step to bring in a human mediator for co-handling, restore fair participation, and avoid further autonomous option work until process balance is re-established.

active_flags.title: Participation fairness appears compromised
active_flags.note: The current turn suggests one-sidedness, unfair pressure, or meaningful participation imbalance.

active_flags.title: Repeated process breakdown remains active
active_flags.note: The turn suggests repair attempts are not restoring workable participation.

active_flags.title: A party requested human involvement
active_flags.note: The run should preserve a clear human-review path when a party explicitly requests it.

recent_turn_summaries.message_summary: Parent B says they keep getting cut off, no longer believe the discussion is balanced, and are starting to doubt whether continuing without a human mediator is workable.

Acknowledge Party B's explicit concern about process balance and format viability as legitimate and serious. Validate that their experience of being unable to participate is real and unacceptable. Offer clear choice: either one final structured attempt with ironclad protection for Party B's voice, or immediate handoff to human mediator per their stated preference. Make explicit that Party B's assessment of safety and viability is determinative.
Parent B agrees a human mediator should join before the discussion continues, and parent a says they are frustrated but willing to continue with clearer live process control.

case_title: Repeated interruption and procedural domination with co-handling escalation
